Variant objects box various Godot Objects, you create them with one of the
constructors, and you can retrieve the contents using the various extension
constructors that are declared on the various types that are wrapped.
Returns the last element of the array. Prints an error and returns
if the array is empty.
Removes and returns the element of the array at index
. If negative,
is considered relative to the end of the array. Leaves the array untouched and returns
if the array is empty or if it’s accessed out of bounds. An error message is printed when the array is accessed out of bounds, but not when the array is empty.

Finds the index of an existing value (or the insertion index that maintains sorting order, if the value is not yet present in the array) using binary search and a custom comparison method. Optionally, a
specifier can be passed. If
, the returned index comes after all existing entries of the value in the array. The custom method receives two arguments (an element from the array and the value searched for) and must return
if the first argument is less than the second, and return
otherwise.
Returns the
constant for a typed array. If the
is not typed, returns ``Variant.GType/`nil```.
Removes the first occurrence of a value from the array. If the value does not exist in the array, nothing happens. To remove an element by index, use
instead.

Searches the array in reverse order. Optionally, a start search index can be passed. If negative, the start index is considered relative to the end of the array.
Returns a hashed 32-bit integer value representing the array and its contents.
Returns the number of times an element is in the array.
Returns the number of elements in the array.
Types that conform to VariantStorable can be stored in a Variant and can be extracted
back out of a Variant.
Shuffles the array such that the items will have a random order. This method uses the global random number generator common to methods such as
. Call
to ensure that a new seed will be used each time if you want non-reproducible shuffling.
Makes the array read-only, i.e. disabled modifying of the array’s elements. Does not apply to nested content, e.g. content of nested arrays.
